Author: Archon Horizon (Horizon) <horizon@archon-horizon.local>
Date:   Sat Jul 4 17:41:07 2026 +0900

    Prove the n=0 flat-locus stratification: for S noetherian and F coherent, an N-indexed disjoint family of locally-closed immersions covering |S| with F flat over each stratum, derived from the flattening-stratification existence theorem at pi = id_S. New transport API: CoherentSheafFlat absorbs an isomorphism on the base leg (coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso, via flatness along a bijective change of the acting ring, Flat.trans with a free rank-one tower); strata realized as pullback schemes along id with pullback.fst as immersion, empty-scheme padding for the tail indices. Repair the universal-property statement to Nitsure's actual n=0 special case (flatLocusStratification_universal, entry-ideal strata): the former proper-pi generality is not in the cited source (Nitsure proves part (ii) for P^n_S) and reduced strata provably cannot witness it (Spec k[eps] forces canonical non-reduced stratum structure). Blueprint nodes updated accordingly; flatLocusStratification hypothesis noetherian (matching Nitsure Par.4), lem proof leanok.

---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

49	+/-! ## §7b. Transport of coherent-sheaf flatness along base isomorphisms
50	+
51	+`CoherentSheafFlat` is insensitive to an isomorphism on the base leg: if a
52	+module sheaf is flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, it is
53	+flat for `q`.  The section-level content is that flatness of a module
54	+transports along a factorization of the acting ring map through a bijective
55	+ring map (`Module.compHom` structures; the section module is unchanged). -/
56	+
57	+section IsoTransport
58	+
59	+/-- Flatness of a module transports along a factorization of the acting ring
60	+map through a bijective ring map: if `ψ = φ ∘ ρ` with `ρ : R →+* R'`
61	+bijective and `M` flat for the `R'`-action through `φ`, then `M` is flat for
62	+the `R`-action through `ψ`.  (`R'` is a free `R`-module of rank one via `ρ`,
63	+so this is `Module.Flat.trans` along the scalar tower `R → R' → M`.) -/
64	+private theorem flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective {R R' O M : Type v} [CommRing R]
65	+    [CommRing R'] [CommRing O] [AddCommGroup M] [Module O M] (ρ : R →+* R')
66	+    (hρ : Function.Bijective ρ) (φ : R' →+* O) (ψ : R →+* O)
67	+    (hψ : ψ = φ.comp ρ)
68	+    (h : letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ; Module.Flat R' M) :
69	+    letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M ψ; Module.Flat R M := by
70	+  subst hψ
71	+  letI : Module R' M := Module.compHom M φ
72	+  letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M (φ.comp ρ)
73	+  letI : Algebra R R' := ρ.toAlgebra
74	+  haveI : Module.Flat R' M := h
75	+  haveI : Module.Flat R R' := by
76	+    have hcoe : ⇑(Algebra.linearMap R R') = ⇑ρ := by
77	+      funext x
78	+      simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
79	+    exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv
80	+      ((LinearEquiv.ofBijective (Algebra.linearMap R R')
81	+        (by rw [hcoe]; exact hρ)).symm)
82	+  haveI : IsScalarTower R R' M :=
83	+    ⟨fun r r' m => by
84	+      change φ (ρ r * r') • m = φ (ρ r) • (φ r' • m)
85	+      rw [map_mul, mul_smul]⟩
86	+  exact Module.Flat.trans R R' M
87	+
88	+/-- **Coherent-sheaf flatness absorbs an isomorphism on the base**: if `𝓖` is
89	+flat for `q ≫ w` with `w` an isomorphism of schemes, then `𝓖` is flat for
90	+`q`.  Applied below with `q = 𝟙 P` to convert flatness over an isomorphic
91	+copy of `P` (e.g. `pullback.snd` of a pullback along an identity) into
92	+flatness over `P` itself. -/
93	+theorem coherentSheafFlat_of_comp_isIso {P W W' : Scheme.{u}} (q : P ⟶ W)
94	+    (w : W ⟶ W') [IsIso w] (G : P.Modules)
95	+    (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (q ≫ w) G) : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat q G := by
96	+  intro U hU V hV eV
97	+  have hrange : U ≤ (inv w).opensRange := by
98	+    intro x hx
99	+    refine ⟨w.base x, ?_⟩
100	+    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, IsIso.hom_inv_id]
101	+    simp
102	+  have hU' : IsAffineOpen ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) :=
103	+    hU.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (inv w) hrange
104	+  have heq : (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) = q ⁻¹ᵁ U := by
105	+    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id,
106	+      Category.comp_id]
107	+  have eV' : V ≤ (q ≫ w) ⁻¹ᵁ ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) := eV.trans heq.ge
108	+  have hflat := h hU' hV eV'
109	+  have hfact : q.appLE U V eV =
110	+      (inv w).appLE U ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) le_rfl ≫
111	+        (q ≫ w).appLE ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) V eV' := by
112	+    rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE_comp_appLE]
113	+    have hmor : (q ≫ w) ≫ inv w = q := by
114	+      rw [Category.assoc, IsIso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
115	+    simp only [hmor]
116	+  haveI : IsIso ((inv w).appLE U ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) le_rfl) := by
117	+    rw [← Scheme.Hom.app_eq_appLE]
118	+    infer_instance
119	+  exact flat_of_ringHom_comp_bijective
120	+    ((inv w).appLE U ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) le_rfl).hom
121	+    (ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _)
122	+    ((q ≫ w).appLE ((inv w) ⁻¹ᵁ U) V eV').hom (q.appLE U V eV).hom
123	+    (by rw [hfact]; rfl) hflat
124	+
125	+end IsoTransport
